title: campanulales
intro: The Campanulales rank is an order of flowering plants characterized by their tubular, bell-shaped or bell-like flowers. This order includes a diverse range of plant families, such as Campanulaceae, Styracaceae, and Lobeliaceae. The Campanulales order also encompasses the families of Gentianaceae and Menyanthaceae, but not the families orchidaceae, euphorbiaceae, or cyperaceae, which belong to other orders. These plants can be found in a variety of habitats, including wetlands, forests, and mountains, and exhibit a range of growth habits, from annuals to perennials.

families.family_desc: Campanulaceae is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the bellflower or harebell family.
